metadata.reason: Desktop thread full transcript export was not available; this file captures the user requests, actions, outputs, blockers, and next steps for durable project memory.

messages.text: Continue the non-Amazon scraping work. Read the Google Sheet of new links, update claim files, remember that catalog model images plus model measurements should be scraped when customer review photos are not available, write prompts for five concurrent threads, and continue scraping in this thread.

Read the Google Sheet and incorporated new candidates into the scrape triage plan. Updated Step 1 intake utilities to include image_source_type and image_source_detail so rows can distinguish customer_review_image from catalog_model_image. Updated the summary/report script so catalog model rows are counted separately.

Completed Catchall partial scrape. Added scrape_catchallstore_reviews.py. Public product pages yielded 14 catalog model rows; catalog endpoints were challenged. Output lives under FWM_Data\non-amazon\data\step_1_raw_scraping_data\catchallstore_com. Claim moved to _claims.

Completed 89th & Madison scrape. Added scrape_89thandmadison_reviews.py using the public Shopify products.json catalog. Scanned 949 products and wrote 789 catalog_model_image rows. Output lives under FWM_Data\non-amazon\data\step_1_raw_scraping_data\89thandmadison_com. Claim moved to _claims.

Completed Victoria's Secret scrape. Added scrape_victoriassecret_reviews.py using the local VSprodLinks catalog plus public Victoria's Secret product and ratings-and-reviews APIs. Scanned 140 product URLs and wrote 1,177 rows: 1,029 customer review image rows and 148 catalog model image rows. Output lives under FWM_Data\non-amazon\data\step_1_raw_scraping_data\vs. Claim moved to _claims.
Checked Free People and marked it blocked_prior_security_challenge_signal based on prior security challenge indicators. No live scrape attempted. Claim moved out of active claims.

Claimed Urban Outfitters in _active_scrape_claims and inspected UO_BigImages.xlsx. The workbook prodLinks sheet had 371 distinct Urban Outfitters product URLs. Product page HTML exposed SSR urbnInitialPiniaState with product data, catalog images, model notes, size/fit measurements, and latest review metadata. Bundled JS revealed the public review API path, but direct probes returned a DataDome interstitial, so the review API was not used.

Added scrape_urbanoutfitters_reviews.py. The scraper reads every URL from the local UO prodLinks catalog, fetches public product pages, parses SSR product state, emits catalog_model_image rows only when a model/catalog image and measurement or model size/fit text are present, and stops on HTTP 403/429 or captcha/interstitial markers.

Ran Urban Outfitters. Intended scope was all 371 workbook product URLs, not aggregate reviews or sample links. The run stopped at the first HTTP 403 per access guardrail. Final output kept rows gathered before that stop: 158 catalog_model_image rows, 0 customer review image rows, all 158 with image, product URL, size, and measurement. Output lives under FWM_Data\non-amazon\data\step_1_raw_scraping_data\urban_outfitters. Claim moved to _claims.

Updated docs: scrape_triage_plan.md, catchallstore_scrape_2026-05-06.md, 89thandmadison_scrape_2026-05-06.md, victoriassecret_scrape_2026-05-06.md, and urbanoutfitters_scrape_2026-05-06.md. Refreshed the non-Amazon merchant rollup to include 101 merchant rows.
good. update the git, S3, and the transcripts table.

Prepared this focused transcript for upload to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table, and planned to sync FWM_Data to S3 and commit the relevant scrape code/docs/transcript changes to Git while avoiding unrelated files modified by other active scraping threads.
